title: Crossing the invisible line: De-differentiation of wake, sleep and dreaming may engender both creative insight and psychopathology.

assets.abstract: Writing about dreaming, the poet Raymond Carver said "I feel as if I've crossed some kind of invisible line". In creative people, the "line" between wake, dreaming and psychopathology may be porous, engendering a de-differentiated, super-critical, hybrid state. Evidence exists for a relationship between creativity and psychopathology but its nature has been elusive. De-differentiation between wake, sleep and dreaming may be the common substrate, as dream-like cognition pervades wake and wake-lik

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Bridge paper score=11.5: links Knowledge/Gnosis concept to mechanistic neuroscience
